pattern: Give non-expert reviewers a bounded role, guided feedback, and permission to flag unclear or hard-to-use wording.
Treat different reviewer backgrounds as different lenses; provide induction and make uncertainty acceptable.

source: PLOS how to review a manuscript
pattern: Separate major from minor issues; comments should say what is wrong, why it matters, and what would fix it.
Require confidentiality, conflict awareness, prompt review, and constructive honesty; warn against unsafe AI/tool sharing.
Map practice questions to NCLEX-PN client needs and entry-level practical nursing judgment; use official materials as framework, not copied qbank content.
Prefer transparent, authoritative, science-based sources and flag unsupported clinical claims.
Review stems and options separately; options should be plausible, parallel, and not cue the answer.
